description_en: A Shiroishi ware ceramic jar, classified as a tangible cultural property (fine arts and crafts). It is municipally designated by the city of Shiroishi, designated on 1982-07-02. Located at 1-chome 7-25 Minamimachi, Shiroishi, Miyagi Prefecture.

A Shiroishi ware sake flask associated with the Sanjin-kō, classified as a tangible cultural property (fine arts and crafts). It is municipally designated by the city of Shiroishi, designated on 2000-03-15. Located at aza Teryashiki-mae 25-6, Shiroishi, Miyagi Prefecture.

name_en: Endō Family and Nakajima Family Documents
description_en: Historical materials of the Endō and Nakajima families, classified as a tangible cultural property (fine arts and crafts). It is municipally designated by the city of Shiroishi, designated on 2011-03-11. Located at Masuoka-chō 2-7, Shiroishi, Miyagi Prefecture.

A statue of Amida Nyorai, classified as a tangible cultural property (fine arts and crafts). It is municipally designated by the city of Shiroishi, designated on 1992-04-01. Located at aza Hommachi 62, Shiroishi, Miyagi Prefecture.

name_en: Image of Shakyamuni and the Sixteen Good Deities
description_en: An image depicting Shakyamuni with the sixteen good deities, classified as a tangible cultural property (fine arts and crafts). It is municipally designated by the city of Shiroishi, designated on 2016-04-19. Located at Ōdaira-nakanome aza Itokuji 1, Shiroishi, Miyagi Prefecture.

name_en: Folding Screen of Shiroishi Castle and Retainers' Battle Standards
description_en: A folding screen depicting Shiroishi Castle and the battle standards of its retainers, classified as a tangible cultural property (fine arts and crafts). It is municipally designated by the city of Shiroishi, designated on 2023-04-13. Located at 1-chome 7-25 Minamimachi, Shiroishi, Miyagi Prefecture.

A painting depicting the nirvana of the Buddha, classified as a tangible cultural property (fine arts and crafts). It is municipally designated by the city of Shiroishi, designated on 2023-04-13. Located at 1-chome 7-35 Minamimachi, Shiroishi, Miyagi Prefecture.

A performing-arts folk tradition of Obara, classified as an intangible cultural property (performing arts). It is municipally designated by the city of Shiroishi, designated on 2001-04-12. Located in the Obara district of Shiroishi.

A kagura sacred dance of the Sakaki school performed in Ōmachi, classified as an intangible cultural property (performing arts). It is municipally designated by the city of Shiroishi, designated on 2006-05-11. Located at Ōtakasawa-Ōmachi, Shiroishi, Miyagi Prefecture.

A solitary Japanese cedar tree at Tochihara, classified as a monument (natural monument). It is municipally designated by the city of Shiroishi, designated on 2002-03-29. Located at Fukuoka-fukaya aza Tochiharayama 156-1, Shiroishi, Miyagi Prefecture.

A solitary Japanese cedar tree at Kessanji, classified as a monument (natural monument). It is municipally designated by the city of Shiroishi, designated on 2002-03-29. Located at 2-chome 213 Minamimachi, Shiroishi, Miyagi Prefecture.

A ginkgo tree at Yukōji, classified as a monument (natural monument). It is municipally designated by the city of Shiroishi, designated on 2002-03-29. Located at Fukuoka-fukaya aza Takanohara 44, Shiroishi, Miyagi Prefecture.

A zelkova tree at Dōgata, classified as a monument (natural monument). It is municipally designated by the city of Shiroishi, designated on 2002-03-29. Located at Fukuoka-kuramoto aza Dōgata 22, Shiroishi.

A Japanese horse chestnut tree at Kamasaki, classified as a monument (natural monument). It is municipally designated by the city of Shiroishi, designated on 2002-03-29. Located at Fukuoka-kuramoto aza Kitsunemine 3-ban 13, Shiroishi.

An edohigan cherry tree at Jōrinji, classified as a monument (natural monument). It is municipally designated by the city of Shiroishi, designated on 2008-07-07. Located at 1-chome 7-35 Minamimachi, Shiroishi, Miyagi Prefecture.

name_en: Main Building of the Former Katta Hospital
description_en: The main building of the former Katta Hospital, classified as a tangible cultural property (building). It is municipally designated by the city of Shiroishi, designated on 2008-07-07. Located at Fukuoka-kuramoto aza Nagamine 14, Shiroishi, Miyagi Prefecture.

name_romaji: Shiroishi-jō (sangai yagura, Ōte ichi-no-mon, Ōte ni-no-mon, dobei, ishigaki)
name_en: Shiroishi Castle (Three-Story Turret, Great Gates, Earthen Walls, Stone Walls)
description_en: Shiroishi Castle, comprising its three-story turret, the first and second main gates, earthen walls and stone walls, classified as a tangible cultural property (building). It is municipally designated by the city of Shiroishi, designated on 2011-07-06. Located at Masuoka-chō 1-4 and elsewhere, Shiroishi, Miyagi Prefecture.

The gate of the Furuyama family residence, classified as a tangible cultural property (building). It is municipally designated by the city of Shiroishi, designated on 2018-09-03. Located at Ōdaira-sakaya aza Nishizaike-mae 27, Shiroishi, Miyagi Prefecture.

name_en: Old Stone Monuments of Minami Anrakuji
description_en: A group of old stone monuments at Minami Anrakuji, classified as a monument (historic site). It is municipally designated by the city of Tagajō, designated on 1973-12-18. Located at Nitta aza Minami Anrakuji, Tagajō, Miyagi Prefecture.

The Fuseishi stone, classified as a monument (historic site). It is municipally designated by the city of Tagajō, designated on 1973-12-18. Located at Ichikawa aza Sakashita, within the Tagajō site, Tagajō, Miyagi Prefecture.

A stone monument dating to the Kōan era, classified as a monument (historic site). It is municipally designated by the city of Tagajō, designated on 2001-11-01. Located at 2-chome Takasaki, Tagajō, Miyagi Prefecture.

The archival documents of the Kikuchi family, classified as a tangible cultural property (historical documents). It is municipally designated by the city of Tagajō, designated on 2005-11-01. Held at the Tagajō City Buried Cultural Property Center, 2-chome 27-1 Chūō, Tagajō, Miyagi Prefecture.

Earthenware bearing an ink inscription reading "Kannonji," classified as a tangible cultural property (archaeological material). It is municipally designated by the city of Tagajō, designated on 2005-11-01. Held at the Tagajō City Buried Cultural Property Center, 2-chome 27-1 Chūō, Tagajō, Miyagi Prefecture.

A title-slip wooden tablet (mokkan), classified as a tangible cultural property (archaeological material). It is municipally designated by the city of Tagajō, designated on 2005-11-01. Held at the Tagajō City Buried Cultural Property Center, 2-chome 27-1 Chūō, Tagajō, Miyagi Prefecture.

name_romaji: Mutsu no kuni koseki kankei urushigami monjo
name_en: Lacquer-Paper Documents Relating to the Mutsu Province Census Registers
description_en: Lacquer-paper documents relating to the census registers of Mutsu Province, classified as a tangible cultural property (archaeological material). It is municipally designated by the city of Tagajō, designated on 2005-11-01. Held at the Tagajō City Buried Cultural Property Center, 2-chome 27-1 Chūō, Tagajō, Miyagi Prefecture.

A tsubo abumi (cup-shaped stirrup), classified as a tangible cultural property (archaeological material). It is municipally designated by the city of Tagajō, designated on 2005-11-01. Held at the Tagajō City Buried Cultural Property Center, 2-chome 27-1 Chūō, Tagajō, Miyagi Prefecture.

A transverse flute (yokobue), classified as a tangible cultural property (archaeological material). It is municipally designated by the city of Tagajō, designated on 2005-11-01. Held at the Tagajō City Buried Cultural Property Center, 2-chome 27-1 Chūō, Tagajō, Miyagi Prefecture.

A sword (katana), classified as a tangible cultural property (archaeological material). It is municipally designated by the city of Tagajō, designated on 2005-11-01. Held at the Tagajō City Buried Cultural Property Center, 2-chome 27-1 Chūō, Tagajō, Miyagi Prefecture.

The archival documents of the Tendō family, classified as a tangible cultural property (historical documents). It is municipally designated by the city of Tagajō, designated on 2012-07-01. Held at the Tagajō City Buried Cultural Property Center, 2-chome 27-1 Chūō, Tagajō, Miyagi Prefecture.

The kuri (temple kitchen and living quarters) of Tennōji, classified as a tangible cultural property (building). It is town-designated by Taiwa, designated on 2014-03-03. Located at Yoshioka aza Tennōji 184-20, Taiwa.

The former residence of the Miyatoko Date family, classified as a tangible cultural property (building). It is town-designated by Taiwa, designated on 1997-01-01. Located at Miyatoko aza Shimokōji 64, Taiwa.

name_romaji: Mokuzō den Shō Kannon Bosatsu zazō
name_en: Wooden Seated Statue Attributed to Shō Kannon Bodhisattva
description_en: A wooden seated statue attributed to the bodhisattva Shō Kannon, classified as a tangible cultural property (fine arts and crafts, sculpture). It is town-designated by Taiwa, designated on 2018-04-01. Located at Ochiai-hōonji aza Uenoyama 31, Taiwa.

name_romaji: Yoshikawa Jūbei no sokuryō kankei shiryō
description_en: Materials relating to the surveying work of Yoshikawa Jūbei, classified as a tangible cultural property (fine arts and crafts, historical material). It is town-designated by Taiwa, designated on 1995-04-01. Not open to the public.

A memorial monument dedicated to Tadaki Shigeyuki, classified as a tangible cultural property (fine arts and crafts, historical material). It is town-designated by Taiwa, designated on 2020-05-01. Located at Yoshida aza Ichinosaka 28, Taiwa.

The hereditary kagura sacred dance of Kanatori, classified as a folk cultural property (intangible folk cultural property, folk performing art). It is town-designated by Taiwa, designated on 1995-04-01. Located in the Kanatori district at Yoshida aza Kanatori-kita and elsewhere, Taiwa.

The site of the former Shingyōji temple, classified as a monument (historic site). It is town-designated by Taiwa, designated on 1995-04-01. Located at Miyatoko aza Matsukurasawa 94-1 and elsewhere, Taiwa.

The former office building of Mizusawa Prefecture, classified as a tangible cultural property (building). It is municipally designated by the city of Tome, designated on 1976-05-27. Located at Toyoma-machi Teraike Sakurakōji 1, Tome, Miyagi Prefecture.

The former residence of the Suzuki family, classified as a tangible cultural property (building). It is municipally designated by the city of Tome, designated on 1989-09-29. Located at Toyoma-machi Teraike Sakurakōji 79, Tome, Miyagi Prefecture.

The Furōsenkan building, classified as a tangible cultural property (building). It is municipally designated by the city of Tome, designated on 1972-10-13. Located at Towa-chō Maiya aza Inari 65, Tome, Miyagi Prefecture.

name_en: Half-Pair Folding Screen "Golden Pheasants" from the Second Bailey of Sendai Castle
description_en: A half-pair folding screen titled "Golden Pheasants" from the second bailey of Sendai Castle, classified as a tangible cultural property (fine arts and crafts). It is municipally designated by the city of Tome, designated on 1978-02-01. Held at the Tome City History Museum, Hasama-chō Sanuma aza Uchimachi 63-20, Tome, Miyagi Prefecture.
